message.content:
POLARIS MISSION (this wheel): Beat the DeepResearch-Bench RACE scoreboard by raising POLARIS report QUALITY. The reliability wheel already proved the deep 328-basket render completes clean + faithful + agentic (that axis is DONE — do NOT chase speed/reliability). The remaining blocker is that RACE quality is UNSTABLE: two runs with BYTE-IDENTICAL config on the SAME corpus scored 0.4447 (step3: 1 outline turn, 3875 words, 11 rich sections including a 'Wage Inequality/Skill Demand' section (470w) and a 'Policy Implications' section (157w), 37 refs) versus 0.3518 (step5: 12 outline turns, 2901 words, only 8 rendered sections — it DROPPED the Wage-Inequality and Policy themes, thinned every body section, and bloated the bibliography to 52 refs). Config parity is byte-identical (reliability-wheel-proven: corpus/RQ/tier-fractions/model/judge all equal), so the whole 0.093 RACE gap is PURE agentic-outline NON-DETERMINISM: more turns produced a WORSE, thinner report. GOAL: make the agentic outline reliably produce the RICH, COMPLETE structure every run so the RACE FLOOR rises to >=0.4447 and climbs toward SOTA (top-3 deep-research systems ~0.45-0.50). The fix MUST be GENERAL (query-agnostic — derive themes from the corpus, never hardcode task-72 sections) and faithfulness is the ONLY hard gate and is NEVER relaxed (strict_verify + NLI + [#calc] lane + fold-in seam stay intact).
SUCCESS IS MEASURED BY: RACE Overall on DeepResearch-Bench task 72 >= 0.4447 REPRODUCIBLY across >=2 clean renders (proving the FLOOR rose, not a lucky draw), each with cp4_used=agentic and faithfulness_pass=true, AND the same fix re-validated on a 2nd DRB task to prove it is general (not task-72 overfit). Stretch: climb the Overall toward SOTA top-3 (~0.45-0.50).
The bar is not "bug fixed" — it is the benchmark number at the top, VERIFIED. Keep going until the
measurement says top (or you hit a hard external blocker you must report honestly).

FENCE (military discipline — no exceptions):
- Work ONLY inside /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent (branch bot/outline-agent-box). NEVER touch another worktree.
- NEVER edit anything under /home/polaris/polaris_project (operator's monitoring dir — deck.sh, wheel*.js, wheels.py).
  If you catch yourself editing a monitoring script or a workflow .js, you have DRIFTED — stop.
- Report REAL output only. Never invent, never claim a pass you did not observe, never fake a benchmark number.
- Commit every landed step in /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent; append one dated line to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md.


YOU ARE FABLE — the scalpel. You are NOT here to read or search the codebase; Opus already did the
hard reading. You are here for ONE precise deep-think: judge what Opus did and decide the next step.

WHAT OPUS DID THIS ROUND:
{
  "did": [
    "READ the real code: outline_agent.run_outline_agent_or_legacy (seed-via-_call_outline then agentic loop), _tool_update_outline, outline_revise.apply_revision_ops merge/split/retitle/reassign/add; multi_section_generator D4 facet-route residual (10416) + route_all orphan routing (10552) + char-budgeted section writer selection.",
    "DIFFED the two run artifacts: step3(0.4447)=9 thematic sections/3230 body words/132 outline ev_ids/37 refs with Wage-Inequality(470w)+Policy(157w) PRESENT; step5(0.3518)=7 body sections + a 533-ev_id residual catch-all/2046 body words/1033 outline ev_ids/52 refs with both themes DROPPED.",
    "IDENTIFIED lever 1 (outline): apply_revision_ops has a required_titles HARD lock but it is EMPTY on the free-form DRB render, so the loop's merge ops collapse distinct corpus themes unconstrained.",
    "IDENTIFIED lever 2 (compose, honest new finding): PG_ROUTE_ALL_BASKETS=1 + PG_EV_BUDGET_TRACKS_PAYLOAD=1 cram 60-95 ev_ids/section into a fixed per-section char budget -> shallower grounding -> strict_verify keeps fewer (61 vs 91) -> thin prose + 52-ref residual dump; step3's 132-ev_id outline means route_all was effectively inert there.",
    "DESIGNED + LANDED Part A: OutlineWorkspace.min_sections = SEED outline section count (query-agnostic corpus theme decomposition), threaded into apply_revision_ops which DEFERS any merge that would net-drop the live section count below the floor. Gate PG_OUTLINE_SECTION_FLOOR default-ON, =0 = byte-identical legacy. Faithfulness-neutral.",
    "WROTE + RAN 6 new unit+seam tests (test_outline_theme_floor.py); all pass, existing 17 outline_revise tests still pass (23/23).",
    "COMMITTED both steps and appended a dated R1 entry to WHEEL_PROGRESS.md."
  ],
  "commits": [
    "03fbb8d",
    "6f893bf"
  ],
  "files": [
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_agent.py",
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/generator/outline_revise.py",
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/tests/polaris_graph/test_outline_theme_floor.py",
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md"
  ],
  "exercised": "Ran tests/polaris_graph/test_outline_theme_floor.py (6) + test_outline_revise.py (17) under the real .env: all 23 PASS. Tests drive actual code: parse_revision_ops -> apply_revision_ops with min_sections, AND the live async _tool_update_outline workspace seam with a real SectionPlan outline_draft. Confirmed floor-ON defers a merge that would collapse 9->8 (Wage-Inequality+Policy preserved); floor-OFF reproduces the collapse; split/add raise the count and are never blocked; reassign applies unblocked; two sequential floor-violating merges both deferred. Both edited modules import cleanly and expose the new min_sections param/field. Real pytest + real async seam invocation, not py_compile.",
  "measurement": "No RACE number this round (ROUND 1 scope = diagnosis + design + cheap exercise; no ~45min render until Fable gates, per the mission ordering). REAL measurements: (1) artifact-level quantification of the gap - step3: 9 sections/3230 body words/132 outline ev_ids/37 refs/91 verified sentences (Wage-Inequality+Policy present); step5: 7 body sections + 533-ev_id residual/2046 body words/1033 outline ev_ids/52 refs/61 verified sentences (both themes dropped). (2) Fix exercised: 23/23 tests pass (6 new theme-floor unit+seam + 17 existing outline_revise, byte-identical at floor=0). Seam test proves floor-ON defers the theme-collapsing merge (9 sections, themes intact) and floor-OFF reproduces the 9->8 collapse.",
  "open": [
    "No RACE measurement yet - ROUND 2 render + score gated on Fable's decision (Part A alone vs Part A + compose de-cramming).",
    "Lever 2 (compose cramming/residual) is designed-only, not landed - needs Fable's faithfulness ruling on capping writer per-section ev-density.",
    "Reproducibility (2 renders) + 2nd DRB task generality not yet run.",
    "If cramming (lever 2) dominates the thinning, Part A may raise section COUNT but not per-section DEPTH - unproven until a render."
  ],
  "status": "PROGRESS",
  "blockers": []
}

OPUS'S PRECISE QUESTION FOR YOU:
Diagnosis found TWO independent levers, not one. Lever 1 (outline): unconstrained merge ops collapse distinct corpus themes - FIXED by Part A (theme floor, commit 03fbb8d, 23/23 tests, faithfulness-neutral). Lever 2 (compose, HONEST finding contradicting the 'pure outline non-determinism' premise): step3's outline carries 132 ev_ids vs step5's 1033 because PG_ROUTE_ALL_BASKETS + PG_EV_BUDGET_TRACKS_PAYLOAD cram 60-95 rows/section into a fixed char budget, thinning every section's grounded prose (61 vs 91 verified) and adding the 52-ref residual dump - Part A does NOT touch this. DECISION: should ROUND 2 render Part A ALONE first (cheapest, isolates whether the merge lever was dominant), or ALSO dial back the compose cramming/residual before the ~45min render - and is capping the WRITER's per-section ev-density (all rows still retained in the pool + bibliography + credibility disclosure; only the writer prompt focuses on the top-N most-credited rows) within the faithfulness 'keep-all / nothing dropped' invariant, or off-limits?



DECIDE, precisely:
1. at_top: does the REAL measurement show top benchmark performance? (RACE Overall on DeepResearch-Bench task 72 >= 0.4447 REPRODUCIBLY across >=2 clean renders (proving the FLOOR rose, not a lucky draw), each with cp4_used=agentic and faithfulness_pass=true, AND the same fix re-validated on a 2nd DRB task to prove it is general (not task-72 overfit). Stretch: climb the Overall toward SOTA top-3 (~0.45-0.50).)
2. correct: is the diff correct for what it claims?
3. faithfulness_safe: are the invariants intact (no derived/unverified number can render; fold-in seam intact)?
4. verdict: SIGN_OFF only if at_top AND correct AND faithfulness_safe. Else CONTINUE (or FIX_REGRESSION if
   Opus broke something). NEVER force-approve. A measurement Opus could not take is NOT a sign-off.
5. next_step: the single most important next action for Opus — precise, file:line where you can.
Think deeply here; this is the one place the wheel spends deep reasoning. Keep it tight and decisive.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

6f893bf WHEEL_PROGRESS: R1 theme-collapse diagnosis + Part A thematic-coverage floor landed+tested

commit 03fbb8d305261913544448453bbd7c69324074a8
Author: polaris-bot <bot@polaris>
Date:   Sun Jul 12 08:45:35 2026 +0000

    RACE-FLOOR: corpus-derived thematic-coverage floor blocks agentic-loop theme collapse
    
    Diagnosis of the RACE 0.4447->0.3518 regression on DRB task 72 (byte-identical
    config): the agentic outline loop SEEDS a rich thematic outline via _call_outline
    (corpus-grounded, query-agnostic) then, over more turns, freely issues merge ops
    that COLLAPSE distinct corpus themes. step3 (0.4447) rendered 9 focused sections
    (3230w) keeping Wage-Inequality(470w) and Policy(157w); step5 (0.3518) ran 12
    turns, merged those themes away to 7 body sections + a residual catch-all (2046w).
    apply_revision_ops has a required_titles HARD lock but it is empty on the free-form
    DRB render, so merges were unconstrained.
    
    Fix (Part A): OutlineWorkspace.min_sections = the SEED outline's own section count
    (a query-agnostic corpus theme decomposition, NOT hardcoded task-72 titles). Threaded
    into apply_revision_ops, which now DEFERS any merge whose net reduction would drop the
    running live-section count below the floor (reason_code=min_sections_floor). split/add
    still raise the count; keep/retitle/reassign are count-neutral. Gate PG_OUTLINE_SECTION_FLOOR
    default-ON; =0 restores byte-identical legacy. Faithfulness-NEUTRAL: pure structural
    placement, strict_verify / NLI / [#calc] lane untouched.
    
    Exercised: 6 new unit+seam tests (test_outline_theme_floor.py) prove the guard defers
    theme-collapsing merges, preserves both themes, allows split/add/reassign, and threads
    through the live _tool_update_outline workspace seam. Existing 17 outline_revise tests
    still pass (byte-identical at min_sections=0).
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 src/polaris_graph/generator/outline_revise.py   |  27 ++++-
 src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_agent.py      |  27 +++++
 tests/polaris_graph/test_outline_theme_floor.py | 125 ++++++++++++++++++++++++
 3 files changed, 178 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

commit 6f893bf626936a5b075e36d72eb89fc9b2e74c9e
Author: polaris-bot <bot@polaris>
Date:   Sun Jul 12 08:46:03 2026 +0000

    WHEEL_PROGRESS: R1 theme-collapse diagnosis + Part A thematic-coverage floor landed+tested

EXERCISED: 6 new unit+seam tests (tests/polaris_graph/test_outline_theme_floor.py) PASS — guard
defers theme-collapsing merges, preserves both themes, allows split/add/reassign, threads through the
live _tool_update_outline workspace seam. Existing 17 test_outline_revise tests still PASS.
NO expensive render yet (per ROUND-1 scope). Awaiting Fable gate on: render Part A alone first, or
also dial back the compose-stage cramming/residual (lever 2) before the ROUND-2 render.

input.description: Show the Part A source diff

content.content:
commit 03fbb8d305261913544448453bbd7c69324074a8
Author: polaris-bot <bot@polaris>
Date:   Sun Jul 12 08:45:35 2026 +0000

    RACE-FLOOR: corpus-derived thematic-coverage floor blocks agentic-loop theme collapse
    
    Diagnosis of the RACE 0.4447->0.3518 regression on DRB task 72 (byte-identical
    config): the agentic outline loop SEEDS a rich thematic outline via _call_outline
    (corpus-grounded, query-agnostic) then, over more turns, freely issues merge ops
    that COLLAPSE distinct corpus themes. step3 (0.4447) rendered 9 focused sections
    (3230w) keeping Wage-Inequality(470w) and Policy(157w); step5 (0.3518) ran 12
    turns, merged those themes away to 7 body sections + a residual catch-all (2046w).
    apply_revision_ops has a required_titles HARD lock but it is empty on the free-form
    DRB render, so merges were unconstrained.
    
    Fix (Part A): OutlineWorkspace.min_sections = the SEED outline's own section count
    (a query-agnostic corpus theme decomposition, NOT hardcoded task-72 titles). Threaded
    into apply_revision_ops, which now DEFERS any merge whose net reduction would drop the
    running live-section count below the floor (reason_code=min_sections_floor). split/add
    still raise the count; keep/retitle/reassign are count-neutral. Gate PG_OUTLINE_SECTION_FLOOR
    default-ON; =0 restores byte-identical legacy. Faithfulness-NEUTRAL: pure structural
    placement, strict_verify / NLI / [#calc] lane untouched.
    
    Exercised: 6 new unit+seam tests (test_outline_theme_floor.py) prove the guard defers
    theme-collapsing merges, preserves both themes, allows split/add/reassign, and threads
    through the live _tool_update_outline workspace seam. Existing 17 outline_revise tests
    still pass (byte-identical at min_sections=0).
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

diff --git a/src/polaris_graph/generator/outline_revise.py b/src/polaris_graph/generator/outline_revise.py
index 88c778e..29ceffd 100644
--- a/src/polaris_graph/generator/outline_revise.py
+++ b/src/polaris_graph/generator/outline_revise.py
@@ -393,6 +393,7 @@ def apply_revision_ops(
     max_recompose_cap: int | None = None,
     outcomes: Sequence[SectionOutcome] | None = None,
     required_titles: Sequence[str] | None = None,
+    min_sections: int = 0,
 ) -> RevisionApplyResult:
     """Apply validated ops deterministically and return the new plan set + the recompose set.
 
@@ -412,7 +413,15 @@ def apply_revision_ops(
     retitled) is skipped as a disclosed no-op (never a ghost recompose title); retitled sections are
     resolved by their CURRENT title. Item 5 (apply half): a NEW title colliding with a live section
     is deferred. Item 12: an ``add`` with no evidence, or a ``reassign`` that empties a section, is
-    marked ``undersupplied=True`` (the gap is disclosed, never faked)."""
+    marked ``undersupplied=True`` (the gap is disclosed, never faked).
+
+    ``min_sections`` (RACE-FLOOR fix, 2026-07-12): the corpus-derived thematic-coverage FLOOR — the
+    minimum number of live sections the outline must retain. A ``merge`` whose net reduction
+    (``len(titles) - 1``) would drop the running live-section count below this floor is DEFERRED as a
+    disclosed no-op (``reason_code=min_sections_floor``). ``split``/``add`` still raise the count and
+    are never blocked; ``keep``/``retitle``/``reassign`` are count-neutral. This stops the multi-turn
+    loop from silently collapsing distinct corpus themes into fewer, thinner sections (the observed
+    RACE 0.4447 -> 0.3518 regression). ``0`` => no floor (byte-identical legacy)."""
     cap = max_recompose_cap if max_recompose_cap is not None else max_recompose()
     base = [_as_dict(p) for p in plans]
     by_title = {p["title"]: p for p in base}
@@ -461,6 +470,11 @@ def apply_revision_ops(
     added_plans: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
     # item 5 (apply half): live section titles (case-insensitive) for the collision guard.
     live_lower: set[str] = {_ci(p["title"]) for p in base}
+    # RACE-FLOOR fix: running count of live sections. merge lowers it (net -(len(titles)-1)),
+    # split/add raise it; keep/retitle/reassign are count-neutral. A merge that would drop this
+    # below ``min_sections`` is deferred (preserves the corpus's distinct thematic sections).
+    live_count = len(base)
+    floor = int(min_sections or 0)
 
     def _recompose_budget_left() -> int:
         return cap - len(recompose)
@@ -503,6 +517,14 @@ def apply_revision_ops(
 
         if kind == "merge":
             titles = [_resolve_current(str(t)) for t in op["titles"]]
+            # RACE-FLOOR fix: a merge that would net-collapse the outline below the corpus-derived
+            # thematic floor is DEFERRED (the loop keeps distinct themes as distinct sections). The
+            # net reduction is (#distinct live sources merged) - 1; count only sources still live.
+            _live_sources = {_ci(t) for t in titles} & live_lower
+            _reduction = max(0, len(_live_sources) - 1)
+            if floor and _reduction and (live_count - _reduction) < floor:
+                deferred.append({**op, "reason_code": f"min_sections_floor:{floor}"})
+                continue
             new_title = str(op["new_title"])
             prospective = live_lower - {_ci(t) for t in titles}
             if _ci(new_title) in prospective:   # item 5: merged title collides with a survivor
@@ -522,6 +544,7 @@ def apply_revision_ops(
                 "archetype": "merged", "undersupplied": not merged_ev,
             })
             live_lower = prospective | {_ci(new_title)}
+            live_count -= _reduction  # RACE-FLOOR: net sections removed by this merge
             recompose.append(new_title)
             applied.append(op)
         elif kind == "split":
@@ -544,6 +567,7 @@ def apply_revision_ops(
                 })
                 live_lower.add(_ci(ct))
                 recompose.append(ct)
+            live_count += max(0, len(child_titles) - 1)  # RACE-FLOOR: net sections added by split
             applied.append(op)
         elif kind == "retitle":
             src_title = _resolve_current(str(op["title"]))
@@ -596,6 +620,7 @@ def apply_revision_ops(
                 "basket_ids": [], "archetype": "added", "undersupplied": not add_ev,
             })
             live_lower.add(_ci(at))
+            live_count += 1  # RACE-FLOOR: an add raises the live section count
             recompose.append(at)
             applied.append(op)
 
diff --git a/src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_agent.py b/src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_agent.py
index 4689809..fb26d5f 100644
--- a/src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_agent.py
+++ b/src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_agent.py
@@ -499,6 +499,17 @@ class OutlineWorkspace:
     # silently add a 5th section behind the caller's back — see `_tool_update_outline` and the
     # auto-assign fallback in `OutlineAgent._execute_tool`.
     required_titles: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
+    # RACE-FLOOR fix (2026-07-12, this wheel): the corpus-DERIVED thematic-coverage floor. The SEED
+    # outline (built by ``_call_outline`` from the evidence/baskets — query-agnostic, NOT a hardcoded
+    # task-72 structure) is the corpus's own theme decomposition. Two byte-identical renders diverged
+    # (RACE 0.4447 vs 0.3518) because the agentic loop, over more turns, freely issued ``merge`` ops
+    # that COLLAPSED distinct corpus themes (e.g. Wage-Inequality, Policy) into fewer, thinner
+    # sections — pure non-determinism that thinned comprehensiveness. This floor caps NET thematic
+    # reduction: the loop may still split / add / retitle / reassign / search (all coverage-improving),
+    # but a ``merge`` that would drop the live section count below the seed count is DEFERRED as a
+    # disclosed no-op. GENERAL (floor = seed's own section count), faithfulness-NEUTRAL (pure
+    # structural placement; strict_verify / NLI / [#calc] lane untouched). 0 => no floor (legacy).
+    min_sections: int = 0
     # Iter-3 P0 fix (real-corpus THINNED run: 8 search_more_evidence calls, 31 genuinely NEW
     # fetched rows, ZERO landed in any required section — the whole retrieval budget was spent

content.content:
# independent (nondeterministic) outline call.
    seed_ev_by_title = {p.title: list(p.ev_ids) for p in parse_result.plans}

    # Iter-2 P2-1 fix (was mis-reported as "new_evidence_count=0" every run): ``OutlineWorkspace``
    # is handed ``ev_store`` BY REFERENCE (not a copy) and the loop mutates it in place via
    # ``search_more_evidence``'s fold-in, so ``final_ws.ev_store is ev_store`` — the SAME dict
    # object. Any comparison of ``len(ev_store)`` computed AFTER the loop against
    # ``len(final_ws.ev_store)`` is comparing an object against itself and is always 0. This also
    # broke the "feed newly-fetched rows back into `evidence`" loop below (the exact same
    # aliasing made every row look like it was "already in ev_store", so it silently fed back
    # ZERO rows — the agent-fetched corpus never reached the caller's `evidence` list at all).
    # Snapshot the id SET before the loop runs; every later comparison is against this snapshot,
    # never against the (now-mutated) live dict.
    ev_ids_before = set(ev_store.keys())
    ev_store_size_before = len(ev_ids_before)

    # Iter-2 fix: thread the deliverable's required-structure lock (if any) through to the
    # workspace so `_tool_update_outline` (via `apply_revision_ops(required_titles=...)`) and the
    # auto-assign fallback both respect it — an exact-N-in-order required structure must never
    # silently grow a 5th section behind the caller's back. Mirrors `_call_outline`'s own
    # `_spec_read(deliverable_spec, "required_sections", [])` read (dict OR object shape).
    _required_titles = [
        str(t).strip()
        for t in (
            deliverable_spec.get("required_sections", [])
            if isinstance(deliverable_spec, dict)
            else getattr(deliverable_spec, "required_sections", [])
        ) or []
        if str(t).strip()
    ]
    # RACE-FLOOR fix: the corpus-derived thematic-coverage floor = the SEED outline's own section

input.gap_to_top: No RACE measurement yet this round (by design). Bar is >=0.4447 reproducibly across >=2 clean agentic+faithful renders on task 72 plus generality on a 2nd DRB task; zero of those renders exist post-fix. Part A is landed and verified (I re-ran 23/23 tests, read the diff — floor logic sound, gated, legacy-identical at 0, faithfulness-neutral), but whether the merge lever was dominant vs the compose cramming (1033 ev_ids / 533-row residual / 61-vs-91 verified) is unproven until a render.
input.next_step: Run ROUND-2 render of Part A ALONE (PG_OUTLINE_SECTION_FLOOR default-ON, no lever-2 changes) on DRB task 72, score RACE, and record artifact stats (section count, per-section words, verified sentences, residual ev_id count, refs). Decision rule: sections>=seed AND body words ~>=3000 AND verified ~>=90 AND RACE>=0.4447 -> proceed directly to reproducibility render #2; if sections restored but prose thin/residual bloated -> land lever 2 (writer top-N ev-density cap) behind its own env gate — RULED faithfulness-safe ONLY if all rows stay in ev_store+bibliography+credibility disclosure, top-N selection is deterministic and disclosed, and strict_verify/NLI still gate every rendered sentence against the full pool; deleting rows from pool/bibliography or weakening any verify gate is off-limits. Lever-2 seam if needed: multi_section_generator writer selection around the char-budget path (~line 10416/10552 region Opus already mapped).
input.reason: Verified with my own tool runs: 23/23 tests pass; diff read line-by-line — merge floor counts only live sources, live_count stays consistent with live_lower, seed-count floor is query-agnostic and yields to required_titles lock; only outline files touched, so strict_verify/NLI/[#calc]/fold-in untouched. Honest no-measurement status means CONTINUE. Part-A-alone render preserves attribution and is diagnostic at the artifact level even under RACE noise; lever 2 is pre-cleared as faithfulness-safe under strict retention+disclosure conditions so Round 3 is unblocked either way.
